chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/236)
text: هُ كَانَ يَقُولُ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي وَ هَذَا النَّهَارَ خَلْقَانِ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَا تَبْتَلِنِي بِهِ وَ لَا تَبْتَلِهِ بِي اللَّهُمَّ وَ لَا تُرِهِ مِنِّي جُرْأَةً عَلَى مَعَاصِيكَ وَ لَا رُكُوباً لِمَحَارِمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ اصْرِفْ عَنِّيَ الْأَزْلَ وَ اللَّأْوَاءَ وَ الْبَلْوَى وَ سُوءَ الْقَضَاءِ وَ شَمَاتَةَ الْأَعْدَاءِ وَ مَنْظَرَ السَّوْءِ فِي نَفْسِي وَ مَالِي

قَالَ وَ مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يَقُولُ حِينَ يُمْسِي وَ يُصْبِحُ رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبّاً وَ بِالْإِسْلَامِ دِيناً وَ بِمُحَمَّدٍ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) نَبِيّاً وَ بِالْقُرْآنِ بَلَاغاً وَ بِعَلِيٍّ إِمَاماً ثَلَاثاً إِلَّا كَانَ حَقّاً عَلَى اللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْجَبَّارِ أَنْ يُرْضِيَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
قَالَ وَ كَانَ يَقُولُ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا أَمْسَى أَصْبَحْنَا لِلَّهِ شَاكِرِينَ وَ أَمْسَيْنَا لِلَّهِ حَامِدِينَ فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ كَمَا أَمْسَيْنَا لَكَ مُسْلِمِينَ سَالِمِينَ
قَالَ وَ إِذَا أَصْبَحَ قَالَ أَمْسَيْنَا لِلَّهِ شَاكِرِينَ وَ أَصْبَحْنَا لِلَّهِ حَامِدِينَ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَمَا أَصْبَحْنَا لَكَ مُسْلِمِينَ سَالِمِينَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Muhammad Bin Ali,

(It has been narrated) raising it to Amir Al-Momineenasws that heasws was saying: ‘(You should be saying), ‘O Allahazwj! Iasws and this day are two creations from Yourazwj creations. O Allahazwj! Neither Involve me with it nor involve it with me, and let not me be seen as audacious upon disobeying Youazwj, nor as indulging in Yourazwj Prohibitions. O Allahazwj! Exchange from me the doom, and the adversities, and the afflictions, and the evil decisions, and the gloating of the enemies, and the evil scenes within myself and my wealth’.

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘And there is none from a servant saying when it is evening and morning, ‘I am pleased with Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> as Lord<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, and with Al-Islam as Religion, and with Muhammad<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> as a Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>, and with the Quran as notification, and with Ali<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> as an Imam<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>’, three times, except that he would have a right upon Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, the Mighty, the Compeller that He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Pleases him on the Day of Judgment’.

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘And he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> was saying when it was evening, ‘In the morning we are grateful to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, and in the evening we are praising ones. So for You<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> is the Praise just as we have come to You<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> in the evening as subbmitters, safe’’.

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘And when it was evening, he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘We have come to the evening as ones grateful to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, and we have come to the morning has ones praising to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, just as we had come to You<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> as submitters, safe’’.
en.sarwar: 12. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Muhammad ibn Ali in a marfu’ manner from Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) who has said the following: “O Lord, I and this day are two creatures of Your creatures. O Lord, do not make me suffer from it and do not make it suffer from me. O Lord, do not allow it see me bold in disobeying You or committing unlawful things. O Lord, remove from me constraint and poverty, affliction and evil determination, and expression of joy from the enemy for bad things in me, and from being placed on an evil position, my property or myself. “The Imam has said, ‘Any servant (of Allah) who says in the morning or evening three times, “I accept that Allah is my Lord, Islam is my religion, Muhammad (a.s.) is the Prophet, the Holy Quran is the text of Divine message and Ali (a.s.) is my Imam,” three times, it will be a right upon Allah, the Majestic, the Compeller to make him happy on the Day of Judgment. ’ “The Imam has said that he (Ali, recipient of divine supreme covenant) would say in the evening, ‘We live in the morning thankful to Allah and we live in the evening praising Allah. All praise belongs to You as we live in the evening submitted to Y our will and in peace. ‘‘The Imam said, ‘He (Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib, recipient of divine supreme covenant) in the morning would say, “We live in the evening thankful to Allah and we live in the morning praising Allah, all praise belongs to Allah. We live in the morning submitted to Your will and in peace.’”
